name: Paystack
description: Leading African payment processor (Nigeria-led, also supports Ghana, South Africa, Kenya, Côte d'Ivoire). Initialize transactions, verify payments, list customers, issue refunds. Test secret keys (`sk_test_*`) require no KYC and work in minutes.
instructions:
This connector uses the Paystack REST API v1.

**Getting test API keys**: sign up at https://dashboard.paystack.com → Settings → API Keys & Webhooks. You get a `sk_test_*` (secret key — server-side, used here) and a `pk_test_*` (public key — for client-side checkout). This adapter only needs the **secret key**.

**Authentication**: Bearer Token. Header: `Authorization: Bearer sk_test_xxxxx`.

**Amount convention**: amounts are integers in the **smallest currency subunit** — for NGN this is **kobo**, so ₦100 = `10000`. Same for ZAR (cents), GHS (pesewas), KES (cents).

**Supported currencies**: NGN (default), USD, GHS, ZAR, KES, XOF (CFA franc).

**Test cards** (test mode only): card number `4084 0840 8408 4081`, CVV `408`, expiry any future date, PIN `0000`, OTP `123456`.

**Webhooks**: real-money flows should verify payments via `paystack_verify_transaction` from a webhook handler, not by polling. The verify endpoint is idempotent and free.

**Production**: requires Paystack KYC (business documents, settlement bank account). Live keys start with `sk_live_` — never commit them.

tools.name: paystack_initialize_transaction
tools.description: Initialize a Paystack transaction. Returns an `authorization_url` you redirect the customer to (Paystack-hosted checkout) and a `reference` you later pass to paystack_verify_transaction. Use this rather than charging cards directly — Paystack handles PCI on their side.

email.description: Customer email (required by Paystack for every transaction).

amount.description: Amount in the smallest currency subunit (e.g. 10000 = ₦100.00 for NGN, 10000 = $100.00 for USD).

reference.description: Optional. Your own reference for this transaction. If omitted, Paystack generates one. Must be unique across the merchant account.

callback_url.description: Optional. URL Paystack redirects the customer to after payment. Overrides the dashboard-configured default.

metadata.description: Optional. Arbitrary key-value object stored on the transaction and echoed in webhooks.

tools.name: paystack_verify_transaction
tools.description: Verify a Paystack transaction by reference. Returns status ('success', 'failed', 'abandoned'), amount, currency, paid_at, channel (card/bank/ussd/qr/mobile_money/bank_transfer), customer object, and authorization details if a card was used. Free, idempotent — safe to call multiple times.

reference.description: The transaction reference returned by paystack_initialize_transaction (or supplied by you).

tools.name: paystack_list_customers
tools.description: List customers on the merchant account. Returns customer id, customer_code, first_name, last_name, email, phone, total_transactions, total_transaction_value, dedicated_account if any, and risk_action.

tools.name: paystack_create_refund
tools.description: Create a refund for a transaction. Refunds are partial by default (use the full amount to do a full refund). Returns refund id, status (pending/processed/processing/failed), transaction object, refunded_at, expected_at, and reason.

transaction.description: Transaction id or reference to refund.

amount.description: Optional. Amount in smallest subunit to refund. Omit to refund the full transaction amount.

currency.description: Optional. Must match the original transaction currency. Defaults to NGN.

customer_note.description: Optional. Note shown to the customer on the refund record (max 100 chars).

merchant_note.description: Optional. Internal note on the refund (max 100 chars).
